concerns.text: FR-008 (Spec) requires including a null model (intercept-only) in the regression analysis. Task T017 (regression_model.py) mentions fitting a model and applying corrections but does not explicitly include a task to implement or test the null model comparison required by FR-008.

FR-005 (Spec) and SC-005 (Spec) mandate applying Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg correction to p-values. While T017 mentions 'Apply Bonferroni/Benjamini-Hoch correction', it lacks a specific sub-task or test to verify this correction is actually applied and recorded in the output, risking non-compliance with the success criteria.

FR-004 (Spec) requires generating 'distance to nearest prime' as a predictor. Task T016 mentions generating this feature, but there is no explicit task to validate that this feature is correctly computed and included in the `features.csv` before the regression step, creating a gap in the data pipeline verification.

SC-003 (Spec) requires exact integer match against reference values for n <= 100. Task T009 only lists specific test values {5, 6, 10, 50, 100}. This is insufficient coverage for the requirement 'n <= 100' which implies a broader range or a specific mechanism to verify the full range, not just a sparse sample.

Task T003 instructs configuring linting in the 'root', but the Plan and Spec define the project structure with `code/` as the source root. This is a minor inconsistency in path specification that could lead to misconfiguration.
